title: CRISPR Failed to Cure HIV. What It Found Instead May Matter More.
description: The first CRISPR therapy ever tested against HIV in humans looked promising — until the virus came back in every patient who stopped antiretrovirals. But the same platform that stumbled on HIV is now producing early remissions in lupus and systemic sclerosis, for a counterintuitive reason: you don't need to permanently edit every cell. You just need to reset the immune system and get out. A rigorous look at what CRISPR can and cannot do, and why the autoimmune pivot may be the field's most important accidental discovery.
heroImageAlt: Editorial illustration for CRISPR Was Supposed to Cut HIV Out of the Human Genome. It Can't — Yet. But the Failure Is Teaching Us Something Stranger.
